diagrams.title: Basic Interrupt System Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows how peripheral devices connect to CPU through an interrupt controller with signal flow
diagrams.purpose: Understanding hardware components and signal flow in interrupt-based I/O systems

diagrams.title: Daisy Chain Priority Scheme
diagrams.description: Shows devices connected in series where position determines priority
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how fixed priority is determined in daisy chain configuration and propagation of interrupt requests

diagrams.title: Interrupt Handling Sequence
diagrams.description: Timeline showing the sequence of events from interrupt request to ISR return
elements: Time 3: Controller raises INTR to CPU
Time 8: CPU fetches ISR from vector table
diagrams.purpose: Understanding the complete interrupt handling process and identifying sources of interrupt latency

diagrams.title: 4-Input Priority Encoder
diagrams.description: Truth table and logic circuit for priority encoding
elements: Enable input and output logic gates
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how combinational logic determines highest priority interrupt request
